decisions.briefing: Markets are in a sharp risk-off selloff with NASDAQ down 4.2% and S&P 500 down 2.6% — one of the worst single-day moves in recent weeks. No screened candidates are available today, which is itself a signal to stay cautious. Holding all six existing positions and keeping powder dry until conditions stabilize.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equity markets sold off sharply on June 5, 2026, with the NASDAQ leading losses at -4.2% as risk appetite evaporated across growth and tech sectors. The broad S&P 500 fell -2.6%, reflecting widespread institutional de-risking as investors rotated out of equities and into safer assets such as bonds and cash. The severity of the NASDAQ decline relative to the S&P 500 suggests the selloff was concentrated in high-beta, growth-oriented names that had led the prior rally.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月5日，美國股市大幅下跌，以科技股為主的納斯達克指數領跌，單日重挫4.2%，標普500指數亦下滑2.6%，市場整體呈現明顯的避險情緒。機構投資者普遍進行去風險操作，資金從股票市場流出，轉向債券及現金等防禦性資產。納斯達克跌幅遠超標普500，反映出此次拋售集中在高貝他值的成長型股票，而這些股票正是此前漲勢的主要動力。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Risk-off is a market condition where investors become fearful and pull money out of higher-risk assets like tech stocks, moving into safer ones like bonds or cash. On a risk-off day, growth stocks tend to fall hardest because they rely on future earnings, which become less valuable when uncertainty rises. Today's NASDAQ drop of 4.2% versus the S&P 500's 2.6% is a classic risk-off pattern — growth gets hit more than value.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指市場投資者因恐懼或不確定性上升，將資金從高風險資產（如科技股）撤出，轉向債券、黃金或現金等相對安全的資產。在避險情緒主導的交易日，成長型股票通常跌幅最大，因為這類股票的估值建立在對未來盈利的預期上，一旦前景不明朗，其吸引力便大幅下降。今日納斯達克跌幅（4.2%）遠超標普500（2.6%），正是典型的避險行情特徵。
